title: Periodic orbits and quantum many-body scars in integrable spin chains
abstract: Quantum many-body scars provide an exception to generic thermalising dynamics, but their relation to integrability remains unclear. Here we apply the periodic-orbit framework to the integrable XYZ/XXZ spin-1/2 chain, developing an energy-resolved approach that enables us to locate and track periodic orbits across the spectrum. We identify families of scarred orbits and resolve their supporting eigenstates in terms of Bethe-ansatz quantum numbers, revealing a common string core dressed by zero-momentum magnon pairs. This allows us to reconstruct both the scarred eigenstates and the associated towers directly within the Bethe ansatz, and explain the equidistant tower spacing analytically from the decoupling of zero-momentum magnon pairs in the Bethe equations, providing a microscopic realisation of scar phenomenology in an integrable setting. We further show that this structure persists upon breaking integrability with a transverse field, with the periodic orbits continuing to govern the dynamics. Our results establish a direct connection between the periodic-orbit picture of quantum scarring and the algebraic structure of integrable models, showing that key features of scar dynamics can be understood analytically within the Bethe ansatz framework.

bite.hook: Researchers have mapped quantum many-body scars directly to periodic orbits in integrable spin chains.
tldr: The authors studied the relationship between quantum many-body scars and integrability in XYZ/XXZ spin-1/2 chains.
They developed an energy-resolved periodic-orbit framework and analyzed eigenstates using Bethe-ansatz quantum numbers.
They analytically explained the equidistant spacing of scar towers and showed these structures persist even when integrability is broken.
bite.whyItMatters: Understanding quantum scars helps physicists find ways to keep quantum systems from losing their information to thermal noise, a key hurdle in building stable quantum computers.
bite.eli5: Usually, a hot soup mixes until it is all the same temperature. Quantum scars are like stubborn cold spots in the soup that refuse to mix, orbiting in a neat, predictable pattern instead of dissolving.

glossary.shortDef: Special high-energy states in quantum systems that resist thermalization and exhibit periodic dynamical behavior.
glossary.eli5Def: Rare, stable states in a chaotic quantum system that refuse to scramble and instead repeat their behavior over and over.

glossary.shortDef: An analytical method for finding the exact wave functions and energy levels of certain one-dimensional quantum models.
glossary.eli5Def: A clever mathematical recipe used to solve complicated quantum problems exactly, instead of just guessing the answers.

glossary.shortDef: A physical system with enough conserved quantities to be solved exactly, preventing chaotic thermalization.
glossary.eli5Def: A physical system that is perfectly orderly and predictable, having enough strict rules that it can never become truly chaotic.
